summary:
Held 1 of today's 10 biggest US movers.

• 5 upside misses — MGM +9%, UAL +6%, NCLH +6%, GM +5%, APTV +5%. These cleared the universe screen but no strategy picked them up; the morning brief either filtered them on a catalyst/RSI/earnings rule or had nothing positive to trigger an entry.

• 4 downside moves (BSX -12%, QCOM -6%, SWKS -6%, BKR -5%) are unreachable on a long-only basket — no mechanism to express a short or pair-trade.

• Most instructive miss: BSX (-12.46%) — CEO Mahoney's Bernstein conference comments disclosed WATCHMAN heart-implant revenue expected flat sequentially in Q2 and Q3 due to declining standalone procedures and rising competitive pressure — surprise guidance cut weeks after Q1 earnings. Why we passed: Outside any active strategy's universe; BSX is a large-cap med-tech name (~$50B market cap) not covered by current sector filters. The -12% move was driven by intraday sell-side conference commentary rather than a pre-scheduled earnings release, so no pre-market signal was available.

top_movers.catalyst: Q1 2026 revenue beat ($1.84B vs $1.78B est) with Q2 guidance above Street; multiple major banks (UBS, Wedbush, Jefferies, Deutsche Bank) raised price targets simultaneously on AI-driven ad platform (Axon) growth.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — APP is a mega-cap ad-tech name whose move was earnings-driven and gap-opened pre-market. Post-open signals arrived too late for momentum entry; macro-aligned flagged it but had no earnings-event entry rule to act on the catalyst.
top_movers.suggestion: Add an earnings-gap rule to macro-aligned: if a name with ≥3 same-day analyst target raises gaps up ≥5% at open on earnings day, allow a half-size entry within the first 30 minutes using the prior session's ATR as the stop.

top_movers.catalyst: Dual same-day analyst upgrades: JPMorgan (Neutral→OW, PT $41→$46) and Truist (Hold→Buy, PT $42→$55), both citing Las Vegas Strip EBITDAR bottoming and improving Q2 room-rate surveys.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — large-cap gaming/hospitality is not covered by current sector filters. Analyst re-rating moves are pre-open catalysts; systematic momentum signals fire after the gap is already priced in.
top_movers.suggestion: Add large-cap gaming names (MGM, CZR, WYNN) to a news-reactive strategy that triggers on ≥2 same-day sell-side upgrades, entering at open only if pre-market gap is ≤5% to avoid chasing.

top_movers.catalyst: CEO Kirby at Bernstein conference (May 27) guided to 53M+ summer passengers (+3M YoY) with double-digit demand/booking growth and easing fuel costs; UBS raised PT to $148 from $139, reiterating Buy.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — UAL is a large-cap airline absent from current sector filters. Move was catalyzed by a sell-side conference appearance + analyst PT upgrade, a pre-market signal type the bot has no scanner for.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a pre-market analyst-event scanner: flag names receiving PT upgrades implying ≥7% upside to prior close and route to a gap-up watchlist at open — regardless of sector — to capture conference-driven moves like this.

top_movers.catalyst: WTI crude fell ~3% on US-Iran Strait of Hormuz deal progress, triggering a broad cruise-sector rally; amplified by residual Elliott activist/Q1 EPS beat ($0.23 vs $0.14 est.) tailwind from May 20.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — NCLH (high-leverage leisure, ~5.3x debt/EBITDA) is absent from all sleeves. Move was a macro sector tail: oil -3% on US-Iran deal news, rewarding pre-open positioning over intraday signals.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a macro-trigger rule: when WTI falls ≥2% intraday, auto-include top-5 high-beta cruise names (CCL, NCLH, RCL, VIK) in the momentum sleeve with a 1-session end-of-day entry gate, bypassing the prior-session volume confirmation filter.

top_movers.catalyst: FDIC conditionally approved GM Financial Bank's industrial loan company charter (Utah), giving GM's captive finance arm access to lower-cost FDIC-insured deposits and expanding financing margins.
top_movers.miss_reason: GM (~$90B market cap auto OEM) is outside every active strategy's universe. The +5.4% move was a binary regulatory catalyst (FDIC bank charter approval) undetectable from price/volume signals — it requires pre-flagging pending regulatory applications to position ahead of the event.
top_movers.suggestion: Build a regulatory-event watchlist for large-cap names with pending FDIC/SEC approvals; trigger a news-reactive entry when the open gap is ≥+3% and intraday volume is ≥1.5× 20-day average on the approval day.

top_movers.catalyst: CEO Mahoney's Bernstein conference comments disclosed WATCHMAN heart-implant revenue expected flat sequentially in Q2 and Q3 due to declining standalone procedures and rising competitive pressure — surprise guidance cut weeks after Q1 earnings.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe; BSX is a large-cap med-tech name (~$50B market cap) not covered by current sector filters. The -12% move was driven by intraday sell-side conference commentary rather than a pre-scheduled earnings release, so no pre-market signal was available.
top_movers.suggestion: Add a conference-day momentum trigger for large-cap names outside the universe: when a stock gaps ≥8% intraday on ≥2× avg volume during a known CEO/CFO conference presentation, flag for a momentum-short entry confirmed within the first 30 min of the move.

top_movers.catalyst: Profit-taking unwind after QCOM surged ~75% on AI enthusiasm (ByteDance chip deal, Stellantis auto pact, CEO's China trip); Q3 FY2026 guidance signalled sequential EPS deceleration to $2.10–$2.30 vs Q2's $2.65, weighing on the broad semiconductor sector.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe — QCOM is a ~$240B large-cap semiconductor not covered by current screens. The move was the second consecutive red session (−7% on May 27, −6.2% May 28) in a mean-reversion unwind from overbought levels, requiring short capability the bot likely does not have.
top_movers.suggestion: Add large-cap semis (market cap >$50B) with 4-week return >35% and RSI >75 to a mean-reversion short watchlist; gate entries on a second consecutive close down ≥3% to confirm exhaustion before sizing in.

top_movers.catalyst: Iran-US peace deal progress triggered a sharp WTI crude sell-off; as an oilfield services supplier, BKR fell ~5.3% on fears that lower oil prices will prompt near-term E&P capex cuts.
top_movers.miss_reason: Outside any active strategy's universe; BKR is a large-cap (~$62B) oilfield services name. The drop was a geopolitical macro shock (Iran-US peace deal → WTI plunge) with no pre-signal available to momentum or mean-reversion filters.
top_movers.suggestion: Add large-cap OFS names (BKR, HAL, SLB) to a sector-rotation strategy gated on WTI 1-day move ≤ −2.5%; this captures macro oil-shock entries without requiring idiosyncratic name-level signals.
